review_execution.command: cat prompt and /tmp/cp00-066-review.diff | claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json

findings.title: Fail-closed (blocked) result still reports next_subphase=RP00.P05.M06.S01 with source_micro_phase_completed=false
findings.details: In assembleControlPlaneSyntheticFixturePermissionAuditHint, next_subphase is always set to policy.nextSubphase (RP00.P05.M06.S01), and assertControlPlaneSyntheticFixturePermissionAuditHintResult hard-requires that exact value even on the deniedTriplet/blocked path where source_micro_phase_completed is false and audit_hint_decision is 'blocked'. A blocked result therefore advertises a handoff to the next micro phase while simultaneously indicating the source micro phase was not completed. The shipped fixture and contract definition both take the allow path; the blocked path is only reachable via test-synthetic upstream input and mirrors the established pattern used by upstream packs (e.g. CP00-065), so there is no security, persistence, or runtime impact. Noted for transparency only.
findings.recommendation: Optional/consistency only: consider decoupling next_subphase from the static policy value on the fail-closed branch (echo the upstream still-open subphase or null) so blocked results do not imply micro-phase completion, or explicitly document that next_subphase is a static handoff pointer decoupled from the decision. No change required for closeout.

residual_risks: Low. The change is metadata-only with no runtime, permission-engine, AuthZ, security-trimming, audit-write, database, storage, idempotency, lock, or product-state side effects; these are enforced both by statically-false result flags and by forbidden-claim rejection in the assembler. The security boundary against raw permission policy / internal audit payload exposure holds: only opaque reference IDs are surfaced and the hint message disclaims payload exposure. Tenant/permission-ref/audit-ref drift is enforced transitively via assertControlPlaneSyntheticFixturePermissionAuditBindingResult on the input rather than re-checked inside the hint assembler, which is acceptable since the input is a previously-validated frozen binding result. Full green from scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs additionally depends on downstream artifacts expected after this review (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-066/* construction-inspection and a CP00-066 entry in docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json or the cp00-066 manifest, plus the RP00.P05.M05.S11 terminal entry in the weighted ledger); these are evidence/planning artifacts, not code defects in this diff.
parsed_review.overall_summary: CP00-066 cleanly adds the RP00.P05.M05.S11 Audit hint case as a metadata-only closeout boundary. The assembler consumes the CP00-065 permission/audit binding result, fails closed on upstream pack mismatch (hard-checks pack_id/planned_pack_id==CP00-065), on missing RP00.P05.M05.S11 upstream handoff, on a prematurely-completed source micro phase, and on any forbidden or unknown boundary claim. It emits a safe metadata-only audit hint that surfaces only opaque permission/audit reference IDs (permission_decision_ref, audit_event_ref, role_check_ref, deny_rule_ref, security_trim_ref) plus a disclaimer message stating raw policy and internal audit payloads are not exposed; no_raw_permission_policy and no_internal_audit_payload are true. All runtime/service-logic/route/UI/fixture-generation/test-runner/golden/permission-engine/AuthZ/security-trimming/audit-ledger/audit-event/database/storage/product-state/idempotency/lock flags are statically false (not input-derived), and no such operations are performed. The result object and its nested arrays are frozen. Contract definition, policy, module, fixture (input+result+result_summary+construction_inspection+fixture_integrity), README, tests, and the validator script are mutually consistent, with the validator live-assembling from both fixture.input and definition.input and asserting byte-equality to the recorded results. Completes source micro phase M05, keeps phase RP00.P05 open, hands off to RP00.P05.M06.S01, and registers RP00.P05.M05.S11 as a terminal weighted boundary. Absent docs/closeout-packs/cp00-066 evidence is expected at review time. Only one non-blocking P3 informational note.
